data.title: BJ's Wholesale Club says it is on solid footing for the back half of the year
data.description:
BJ's Wholesale Club Holdings (NYSE:BJ) traded slightly lower in early action on Friday after the warehouse club chain posted a mixed Q2 earnings report.

Total comparable club sales decreased by 0.3% during the quarter due to declining retail prices of fuel. Excluding the impact of gasoline sales, comparable club sales increased by 2.3%. Top-line revenue of $5.38 billion fell short of the consensus estimate by $100 million.

Membership fee income increased 9.0% year-over-year to $123.3 million in Q2. The increase was primarily driven by strength in membership acquisition, retention, and higher-tier membership penetration across both new and existing clubs, as well as the increase in annual membership fees, which became effective in January 2025.

Merchandise gross margin rate increased by 20 basis points compared to the first six months of fiscal 2024. The company noted that it continues to manage the business to drive profitable growth across the broader merchandise assortment. Operating income was up 6.3% to $216.5 million. Non-GAAP EPS was reported at $1.14 vs. $1.09 consensus and $1.09 a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA rose 8.0% to $303.9 million.

"Our business model continues to perform and build upon momentum, as we grow membership and gain market share even in a dynamic environment. We enter the back half of the year on solid footing and confident in our ability to deliver strong results," stated CEO Bob Eddy.

Looking ahead, BJ's (NYSE:BJ) sees comparable club sales, excluding the impact of gasoline sales, increasing 2.0% to 3.5% year-over-year for the full year. Adjusted EPS is seen landing in a range from $4.20 to $4.35 (midpoint $4.275) vs. $4.30 consensus.

Shares of BJ's (BJ) slipped 0.5% in premarket trading to $106.00 vs. the 52-week range of $76.33 to $121.10. The warehouse club stock is still up more than 18% on a year-to-date basis.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, Dow futures rise ahead of Powell's speech at Jackson Hole
data.description:
Stock index futures ticked up on Friday as investors awaited Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell's speech at Jackson Hole. 

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.3%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.3%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.31%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.80%.

Wall Street closed lower on Thursday, pressured by declining shares of Walmart (WMT).

“The theme of 'good news is bad news' returned to markets yesterday following a strong US PMI release. This led investors to dial back expectations of Fed rate cuts…that leaves investors in a jittery mood going into the Jackson Hole Symposium,” Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said. 

The main event for the day will be Powell's speech. He will speak on “Economic Outlook and Framework Review” before the 2025 Jackson Hole Economic Policy Symposium. Traders will hope to get any clues about the monetary policy outlook. Markets are expecting a rate cut in September. 

“Investors will be keenly watching whether Powell places more emphasis on weaker payrolls versus more stable measures of labor market slack and still solid activity and inflation data,” Sidorov added. 

Powell's speech is slated for 10 am ET. 

“Powell has lacked confidence and failed to articulate a medium-term vision for the economy at a time of uncertainty caused by structural change. The resulting mantra of 'data dependency' has not been helpful, as data has very obviously become significantly less reliable,” UBS' Paul Donovan said.

Today’s speech by Powell in theory looks at the economic outlook. It is to be hoped it will offer a robust defense of central bank independence too, Donovan added.

data.title: Ubiquiti raises dividend by 33.3% to $0.80 a share; announces new stock repurchase up to $500M
data.description:
• Ubiquiti (NYSE:UI) declares $0.80/share quarterly dividend, 33.3% increase from prior dividend of $0.60.
  • Forward yield 0.82%
  • Payable Sept. 8; to shareholders of record Sept. 2; ex-div Sept. 2.
  • The company has initiated a new stock repurchase program authorizing the company to repurchase up to $500 million of its common stock, as disclosed in the Form 8-K filed on August 22, 2025.
  • See UI Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Personalis gains as VA awards work order worth up to $13.5M
data.description:
Shares of Personalis (NASDAQ:PSNL) added ~5% in the premarket on Friday after the cancer test maker announced that it received a new task order worth up to $13.5M under a contract with the Department of Veterans Affairs.

The maker of the NeXT Personal cancer test offers genome sequencing services as part of a contract with the Department of Veterans Affairs Million Veteran Program (VA MVP), a large-scale population sequencing initiative.

The new task order, offered under the VA MVP program, takes effect on September 30, 2025, subject to the receipt of samples and the fulfillment of services by the company, Personalis (NASDAQ:PSNL) said in a regulatory filing on Friday.

To date, the company has received nearly $225 million in task orders under its contracts with the VA MVP, which accounted for 9% of its topline in 2024 and 13% in 2023, respectively.

data.title: Buckle GAAP EPS of $0.89 beats by $0.06, revenue of $305.7M beats by $13.09M
data.description:
• Buckle press release (NYSE:BKE): Q2 GAAP EPS of $0.89 beats by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $305.7M (+8.3% Y/Y) beats by $13.09M.
  • Comparable store net sales for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended August 2, 2025, increased 7.3 percent from comparable store net sales for the prior year 13-week period ended August 3, 2024.
  • Online sales increased 17.7 percent to $43.6 million for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended August 2, 2025, compared to net sales of $37.0 million for the 13-week fiscal quarter ended August 3, 2024.

data.title: Fortinet boosts share repurchase program by $1B
data.description:
• Fortinet (NASDAQ:FTNT) has increased its share repurchase program by $1 billion, bringing its aggregate authorized repurchase amount to up to $9.25B.
  • The program was also extended through February 28, 2027. As of August 21, 2025, approximately $1.23B remained available under the existing program, including the $1B increase.

data.title: Hyperscale Data reaffirms 2025 guidance, citing Gresham Worldwide's emergence
BJ’s Wholesale Club reports mixed Q2 results; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• BJ’s Wholesale Club press release (NYSE:BJ): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.14 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $5.38B (+3.3% Y/Y) misses by $100M.
  • Comparable club sales decreased by 0.3% year-over-year due to declining retail prices of fuel.
  • Comparable club sales, excluding gasoline sales, increased by 2.3% year-over-year, led by traffic growth.
  • Membership fee income increased by 9.0% year-over-year to $123.3 million.
  • Member count grew to a record 8 million members.
  • Digitally enabled comparable sales growth was 34%, reflecting two-year stacked comp growth of 56%.

  • The Company provided an updated outlook for fiscal 2025:

    • Comparable club sales, excluding the impact of gasoline sales, to increase 2.0% to 3.5% year-over-year
    • Adjusted EPS to range from $4.20 to $4.35 (prior $4.10 to $4.30) vs $4.31 consensus.
    • Capital expenditures of approximately $800 million

data.title: Bally's lands key regulatory approvals related to the Intralot S.A. deal
data.description:
Bally's Corporation (NYSE:BALY) updated investors on the progress on its previously disclosed transaction agreement with Intralot S.A. (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY).

Under the terms of the deal, Intralot (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY) is set to acquire all issued and outstanding capital stock of Bally's (NYSE:BALY) Holdings Limited. That subsidiary holds Bally's (BALY) international interactive business. The expected closing of the transaction in Q4 will result in Bally’s (BALY) becoming the majority shareholder of Intralot. The deal is structured so that while Intralot takes direct ownership of the international interactive assets, control of the combined global entity shifts to Bally's (BALY), meaning it will have influence over Intralot's broader operations.

On August 14, Bally's (BALY) received notifications from the Gambling Commission of Great Britain confirming that, contingent on routine ongoing requirements, its UK licenses (held by Gamesys Limited, Bally’s (Newcastle) Limited, and Gamesys Operations Limited) will remain in effect after the transaction closes. The regulatory approval addresses a critical hurdle and clears a path for finalizing the transaction.

The deal with Intralot (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY) is seen by analysts as transformative for Bally's (BALY) which has been repositioning itself as a global digital gaming and interactive entertainment player. By integrating with Intralot’s established international operations and technology, Bally’s (BALY) expects to broaden its global reach and strengthen its digital platform, especially in regulated European and emerging markets. 

Intralot (OTCPK:IRLTF) (OTC:IRLTY) is a Greek-based, publicly listed global gaming technology company that provides gambling systems, sports betting management, interactive gaming services, and omnichannel lottery solutions to state-licensed operators in over 39 countries. The company is best known for lottery modernization, digital transformation, and responsible gaming.

Shares of Bally's (BALY) were down 1.6% in premarket trading on Friday.
